q: Who was the first judge of Israel?
e: Judges 3:9 — The Lord raised up a deliverer for the Israelites, Othniel son of Kenaz.

q: Who was the only female judge?
e: Judges 4:4 — Now Deborah, a prophet, the wife of Lappidoth, was leading Israel.

q: Which judge killed 600 Philistines with an oxgoad?
e: Judges 3:31 — After Ehud came Shamgar... who struck down six hundred Philistines with an oxgoad.

q: What was the name of the woman who killed the Canaanite general Sisera?
e: Judges 4:21 — But Jael, Heber’s wife... drove the tent peg through his temple into the ground.

q: How many men were in Gideon's final army?
e: Judges 7:7 — The Lord said to Gideon, 'With the three hundred men... I will save you.'

q: What sign did Gideon ask of God to confirm his call?
e: Judges 6:37 — 'I will place a wool fleece on the threshing floor.'

q: Which judge made a rash vow that led to the sacrifice of his daughter?
e: Judges 11:30-31 — Jephthah made a vow to the Lord... 'whatever comes out of the door of my house...'

q: Who was Samson's father?
e: Judges 13:2 — A certain man of Zorah, named Manoah...

q: What animal did Samson kill with his bare hands?
e: Judges 14:6 — The Spirit of the Lord came powerfully upon him so that he tore the lion apart.

q: What did Samson use to kill 1,000 Philistines?
e: Judges 15:15 — Finding a fresh jawbone of a donkey... he struck down a thousand men.

q: Who was the woman who betrayed Samson to the Philistines?
e: Judges 16:4-18 — Delilah... said to him, 'Tell me the secret of your great strength.'

q: How did Samson die?
o: He was killed by a sword
He was hung from a tree
He pulled down the pillars of a temple
e: Judges 16:30 — He pushed with all his might, and down came the temple.

q: Which judge was a left-handed man?
e: Judges 3:15 — The Lord gave them a deliverer—Ehud, a left-handed man.

q: Who was the commander of the Israelite army during Deborah's time?
e: Judges 4:6 — She sent for Barak son of Abinoam...

q: What did Gideon's 300 men carry in their hands when they attacked the Midianites?
e: Judges 7:16 — He placed trumpets and empty jars in the hands of all of them, with torches inside.

q: Who was the king of Moab that Ehud killed?
e: Judges 3:17 — Ehud presented the tribute to Eglon king of Moab.

q: Which judge was the son of a prostitute?
e: Judges 11:1 — Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty warrior. His father was Gilead; his mother was a prostitute.

q: How many Philistines did Samson kill at his death?
o: More than he killed in his lifetime
e: Judges 16:30 — Thus he killed many more when he died than while he lived.

q: What was the secret of Samson's strength?
e: Judges 16:17 — 'No razor has ever been used on my head... I have been a Nazirite.'

q: Which judge was found threshing wheat in a winepress?
e: Judges 6:11 — Gideon was threshing wheat in a winepress to keep it from the Midianites.

q: Who was Gideon's son who killed his seventy brothers?
e: Judges 9:5 — He went to his father’s home at Ophrah and on one stone murdered his seventy brothers.

q: How did Abimelech die?
o: A woman dropped a millstone on his head
e: Judges 9:53 — A woman dropped an upper millstone on his head and cracked his skull.

q: What was the recurring theme in the book of Judges?
o: Everyone did what was right in their own eyes
e: Judges 21:25 — In those days Israel had no king; everyone did as they saw fit.

q: How many years was Samson a judge over Israel?
e: Judges 15:20 — Samson led Israel for twenty years in the days of the Philistines.

q: Who was the judge who had thirty sons who rode thirty donkeys?
e: Judges 10:4 — He had thirty sons, who rode thirty donkeys.

q: Who was the judge from Bethlehem who had thirty sons and thirty daughters?
e: Judges 12:8-9 — Ibzan of Bethlehem... had thirty sons and thirty daughters.

q: Which judge served for ten years and was buried in Aijalon?
e: Judges 12:11-12 — Elon the Zebulunite led Israel ten years.

q: Who was the judge who had forty sons and thirty grandsons who rode on seventy donkeys?
e: Judges 12:13-14 — Abdon son of Hillel... had forty sons and thirty grandsons, who rode on seventy donkeys.

q: What did Samson find in the carcass of the lion he had killed?
o: A swarm of bees and honey
e: Judges 14:8 — He turned aside to look at the lion's carcass, and in it he saw a swarm of bees and some honey.

q: What was the riddle Samson posed to the Philistines?
o: Out of the eater, something to eat; out of the strong, something sweet.
What is stronger than a lion?
Who can find a virtuous woman?
e: Judges 14:14 — He said to them, 'Out of the eater, something to eat; out of the strong, something sweet.'

q: How many foxes did Samson catch to burn the Philistines' fields?
e: Judges 15:4 — So he went out and caught three hundred foxes.

q: Which judge was from the tribe of Issachar and lived in Shamir?
e: Judges 10:1 — After the time of Abimelech, a man of Issachar named Tola... rose to save Israel.

q: Who was the father of Gideon?
e: Judges 6:11 — Gideon son of Joash...

q: What did the people of Israel worship after Gideon died?
e: Judges 8:33 — No sooner had Gideon died than the Israelites... set up Baal-Berith as their god.

q: What was the name of the valley where Delilah lived?
e: Judges 16:4 — Some time later, he fell in love with a woman in the Valley of Sorek whose name was Delilah.

q: How many times did Delilah try to bind Samson before he told her the truth?
e: Judges 16:6-15 — She tried with fresh thongs, new ropes, and weaving his hair before he told her the truth.

q: What was the name of the Philistine god in whose temple Samson was brought?
e: Judges 16:23 — Now the rulers of the Philistines assembled to offer a great sacrifice to Dagon their god.

q: What was the password used by the Gileadites to identify the Ephraimites?
e: Judges 12:6 — If he said, 'Sibboleth,' because he could not pronounce the word correctly...

q: Who was the king of the Canaanites during the time of Deborah?
e: Judges 4:2 — The Lord sold them into the hands of Jabin king of Canaan.

q: What was the name of the place where Gideon built an altar to the Lord?
e: Judges 6:24 — So Gideon built an altar to the Lord there and called it The Lord Is Peace.

q: How many years was Israel in the hands of the Philistines before Samson?
e: Judges 13:1 — Again the Israelites did evil... so the Lord delivered them into the hands of the Philistines for forty years.

q: Which judge had a mother who was a 'prophetess'? (Wait, actually Deborah was the prophetess/judge). Let's rephrase: Who was the judge associated with the city of Gilead?
e: Judges 11:1 — Jephthah the Gileadite...

q: What was the name of Gideon's other name?
e: Judges 6:32 — So because Gideon broke down Baal's altar, they gave him the name Jerub-Baal.

q: Which judge's name means 'Strong'? (Wait, Shamgar is often associated with that, but let's stick to facts). Who was the judge who led Israel after Tola?
e: Judges 10:3 — He was followed by Jair of Gilead, who led Israel twenty-two years.

q: How many years did the land have peace after Ehud?
e: Judges 3:30 — That day Moab was made subject to Israel, and the land had peace for eighty years.

q: What did the angel of the Lord do to the meat and bread Gideon prepared?
o: Touched it with a staff and fire consumed it
Told him to throw it away
e: Judges 6:21 — With the tip of the staff... the angel of the Lord touched the meat and the unleavened bread. Fire flared from the rock, consuming the meat and the bread.

q: Who was the only judge to have his birth announced by an angel?
e: Judges 13:3 — The angel of the Lord appeared to her and said... 'you will become pregnant and have a son.'

q: What did Jael give Sisera to drink?
e: Judges 4:19 — 'I’m thirsty,' he said. 'Please give me some water.' She opened a skin of milk and gave him a drink.

q: How many of Gideon's men left because they were afraid?
e: Judges 7:3 — Twenty-two thousand men left, while ten thousand remained.

q: What was the name of the place where the Israelites wept because the angel of the Lord rebuked them?
e: Judges 2:1-5 — They called that place Bokim (meaning 'weepers') and offered sacrifices there.
